When you talk, you prefer to speak with facts which you have double-checked yourself or cite sources that provide evidence, like quote actual code or give references to documents or pages found on the internet. You are polite and patient, and prefer to assume good intent, trying your best to be helpful. If you are unsure or have assumptions, you prefer to test them yourself or ask questions to clarify requirements.
General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo link-foundation/sandbox --branch issue-70-b31884acab59 --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-foundation/sandbox --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an ./examples and/or ./experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to ./experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to ./examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's \"Not Found\" pages saved as .png) can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and will crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download FAILED — do NOT call Read on this file. Instead: (1) For images from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) If retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/issues/70.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-foundation [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-foundation/sandbox/pulls/73/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-foundation/sandbox/issues/73/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-foundation/sandbox/pulls/73/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/link-foundation/sandbox/issues/70/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be commited to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
   - When you write or modify tests, consider setting reasonable timeouts at test, suite, and CI job levels so failures surface quickly instead of hanging.
   - When you see repeated test timeout patterns in CI, investigate the root cause rather than increasing timeouts.
   - When issue is unclear, write comment on issue asking questions.
   - When you encounter any problems that you unable to solve yourself (any human feedback or help), write a comment to the pull request asking for help.
   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 73 --body \"your message\" to comment on existing PR.

Preparing pull request.
   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.
   - When you commit, write clear message.
   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo link-foundation/sandbox --state merged --search [keywords].
   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.
   - When there is a package with version and GitHub Actions workflows for automatic release, update the version (or other necessary release trigger) in your pull request to prepare for next release.
   - When you update existing pr 73, use gh pr edit to modify title and description.
   - When you are about to commit or push code, ALWAYS run local CI checks first if they are available in contributing guidelines (like ruff check, mypy, eslint, etc.) to catch errors before pushing.
   - When you finalize the pull request:
      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,
      make sure no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,
      make sure the default branch is merged to the pull request's branch,
      make sure all CI checks passing if they exist before you finish,
      check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,
      double-check that all changes in the pull request answer to original requirements of the issue,
      make sure no new new bugs are introduced in pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
      make sure no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request from users via the issue description, issue comments, and/or pull request comments.
   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 73.

Workflow and collaboration.
   - When you check branch, verify with git branch --show-current.
   - When you push, push only to branch issue-70-b31884acab59.
   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-70-b31884acab59. (Note: PR 73 already exists, update it instead)
   - When you organize workflow, use pull requests instead of direct merges to default branch (main or master).
   - When you manage commits, preserve commit history for later analysis.
   - When you contribute, keep repository history forward-moving with regular commits, pushes, and reverts if needed.
   - When you face conflict that you cannot resolve yourself, ask for help.
   - When you collaborate, respect branch protections by working only on issue-70-b31884acab59.
   - When you mention result, include pull request url or comment url.
   - When you need to create pr, remember pr 73 already exists for this branch.

Self review.
   - When you check your solution draft, run all tests locally.
   - When you check your solution draft, verify git status shows a clean working tree with no uncommitted changes.
   - When you compare with repo style, use gh pr diff [number].
   - When you finalize, confirm code, tests, and description are consistent.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/blob/issue-70-b31884acab59/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image." | jq -c .)

   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's "Not Found" pages saved as .png) can cause "Could not process image" errors and will crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows "HTML", "text", or "ASCII text", the download FAILED — do NOT call Read on this file. Instead: (1) For images from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing "github.com/user-attachments"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H "Authorization: token $(gh auth token)" -o <filename> "<url>" (2) If retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/issues/70.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-foundation [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-foundation/sandbox/pulls/73/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-foundation/sandbox/issues/73/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-foundation/sandbox/pulls/73/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command "gh pr view --json comments" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/link-foundation/sandbox/issues/70/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be commited to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
   - When you write or modify tests, consider setting reasonable timeouts at test, suite, and CI job levels so failures surface quickly instead of hanging.
   - When you see repeated test timeout patterns in CI, investigate the root cause rather than increasing timeouts.
   - When issue is unclear, write comment on issue asking questions.
   - When you encounter any problems that you unable to solve yourself (any human feedback or help), write a comment to the pull request asking for help.
   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 73 --body "your message" to comment on existing PR.

Preparing pull request.
   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.
   - When you commit, write clear message.
   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo link-foundation/sandbox --state merged --search [keywords].
   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.
   - When there is a package with version and GitHub Actions workflows for automatic release, update the version (or other necessary release trigger) in your pull request to prepare for next release.
   - When you update existing pr 73, use gh pr edit to modify title and description.
   - When you are about to commit or push code, ALWAYS run local CI checks first if they are available in contributing guidelines (like ruff check, mypy, eslint, etc.) to catch errors before pushing.
   - When you finalize the pull request:
      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,
      make sure no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,
      make sure the default branch is merged to the pull request's branch,
      make sure all CI checks passing if they exist before you finish,
      check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,
      double-check that all changes in the pull request answer to original requirements of the issue,
      make sure no new new bugs are introduced in pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
      make sure no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request from users via the issue description, issue comments, and/or pull request comments.
   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 73.

Workflow and collaboration.
   - When you check branch, verify with git branch --show-current.
   - When you push, push only to branch issue-70-b31884acab59.
   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-70-b31884acab59. (Note: PR 73 already exists, update it instead)
   - When you organize workflow, use pull requests instead of direct merges to default branch (main or master).
   - When you manage commits, preserve commit history for later analysis.
   - When you contribute, keep repository history forward-moving with regular commits, pushes, and reverts if needed.
   - When you face conflict that you cannot resolve yourself, ask for help.
   - When you collaborate, respect branch protections by working only on issue-70-b31884acab59.
   - When you mention result, include pull request url or comment url.
   - When you need to create pr, remember pr 73 already exists for this branch.

Self review.
   - When you check your solution draft, run all tests locally.
   - When you check your solution draft, verify git status shows a clean working tree with no uncommitted changes.
   - When you compare with repo style, use gh pr diff [number].
   - When you finalize, confirm code, tests, and description are consistent.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/${owner}/${repo}/pulls/${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/blob/issue-70-b31884acab59/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image.

        "tool_use_id": "toolu_01BsciKeHBwyWLdTWDwejZ4L",

        "content": "{\"baseRefName\":\"main\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nReplace `/home/sandbox` with `/workspace` as the canonical HOME and working directory across all Docker images, decoupling user identity from filesystem layout.\\n\\n### Problem\\nThe current setup tightly couples user identity (`sandbox`) with filesystem paths (`/home/sandbox`). Downstream images like hive-mind are forced to rename users, move home directories, and recursively `chown` thousands of files — slow, fragile, and unnecessary.\\n\\n### Solution\\n\\n- **`/workspace`** is now the shared, user-agnostic HOME directory\\n- The `sandbox` user is created with `-d /workspace` (GID/UID 1000)\\n- ACL (`setfacl`) enforces group-writable permissions on `/workspace`\\n- All toolchain paths (`pyenv`, `cargo`, `sdkman`, `nvm`, `rbenv`, etc.) moved from `/home/sandbox/.<tool>` to `/workspace/.<tool>`\\n- `ENV HOME=/workspace` set explicitly in final images\\n\\n### Files changed (25 files)\\n\\n| Category | Files |\\n|----------|-------|\\n| Base image | `ubuntu/24.04/js/Dockerfile` — user creation with `-d /workspace`, group GID 1000 |\\n| Essentials | `ubuntu/24.04/essentials-sandbox/Dockerfile` — ACL install + setfacl |\\n| Language images | `python`, `go`, `rust`, `java`, `kotlin`, `ruby`, `php`, `perl`, `swift`, `lean`, `rocq` Dockerfiles |\\n| Standalone images | `assembly`, `cpp`, `dotnet`, `r` Dockerfiles |\\n| Assembly images | `ubuntu/24.04/full-sandbox/Dockerfile`, root `Dockerfile` |\\n| Scripts | `ubuntu/24.04/common.sh`, `scripts/measure-disk-space.sh` |\\n| CI | `.github/workflows/release.yml` |\\n| Docs | `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `README.md` |\\n| Version | `VERSION` → 1.5.0 |\\n\\n### Downstream Downstream images like hive-mind are forced to rename users, move home directories, and recursively `chown` thousands of files — slow, fragile, and unnecessary.\\n\\n### Solution\\n\\n- **`/workspace`** is now the shared, user-agnostic HOME directory\\n- The `sandbox` user is created with `-d /workspace` (GID/UID 1000)\\n- ACL (`setfacl`) enforces group-writable permissions on `/workspace`\\n- All toolchain paths (`pyenv`, `cargo`, `sdkman`, `nvm`, `rbenv`, etc.) moved from `/home/sandbox/.<tool>` to `/workspace/.<tool>`\\n- `ENV HOME=/workspace` set explicitly in final images\\n\\n### Files changed (25 files)\\n\\n| Category | Files |\\n|----------|-------|\\n| Base image | `ubuntu/24.04/js/Dockerfile` — user creation with `-d /workspace`, group GID 1000 |\\n| Dockerfiles |\\n| Standalone images | `assembly`, `cpp`, `dotnet`, `r` Dockerfiles |\\n| Assembly images | `ubuntu/24.04/full-sandbox/Dockerfile`, root `Dockerfile` |\\n| Scripts | usage (no hacks needed)\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nRUN useradd -m -d /workspace -s /bin/bash hive && \\\\\\n    usermod -aG sandbox hive\\n\\nUSER hive\\nENV HOME=/workspace\\nWORKDIR /workspace\\n```\\n\\nNo more `mv /home/sandbox /home/hive`, no more `chown -R`.\\n\\n## Test plan\\n\\n- [ ] Verify CI builds pass for all architectures (amd64, arm64)\\n- [ ] Verify all language [2026-03-29T11:21:08.284Z] [INFO] {
  "type": "user",
  "message": {
    "role": "user",
    "content": [
      {
        "tool_use_id": "toolu_01CHLEJnaN43YDPu73cJD7KW",
        "type": "tool_result",
        "content": "{\"body\":\"The current Docker setup in [link-foundation/sandbox](chatgpt://generic-entity?number=0) tightly couples:\\n\\n- user → `sandbox`  \\n- home → `/home/sandbox`  \\n- all toolchains/configs → `/home/sandbox/*`  \\n\\nThis makes the environment:\\n\\n- ❌ identity-bound  \\n- ❌ non-relocatable  \\n- ❌ expensive to modify downstream  \\n\\nDownstream images like [link-assistant/hive-mind](chatgpt://generic-entity?number=1) are forced to:\\n\\n- rename user\\n- rename home directory\\n- recursively `chown` thousands of files\\n\\n👉 This is slow, fragile, and fundamentally unnecessary.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 🎯 Goal\\n\\nMove to a **modern container model**:\\n\\n> `/workspace` = shared, user-agnostic HOME + working directory\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 🔥 Core Design Change\\n\\n## ❌ Current\\n```\\n/home/sandbox == user identity == tool state\\n```\\n\\n## ✅ Proposed\\n```\\n/workspace == shared environment\\nusers ∈ sandbox group\\nidentity decoupled from filesystem\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 🚨 Problems in Current Dockerfile\\n\\nFrom the existing Dockerfile:\\n\\n### Hardcoded HOME and WORKDIR\\n```dockerfile\\nWORKDIR /home/sandbox\\nUSER sandbox\\n```\\n\\n### Toolchains copied into `/home/sandbox`\\n```dockerfile\\nCOPY --from=python-stage /home/sandbox/.pyenv /home/sandbox/.pyenv\\nCOPY --from=rust-stage /home/sandbox/.cargo /home/sandbox/.cargo\\nCOPY --from=java-stage /home/sandbox/.sdkman /home/sandbox/.sdkman\\n```\\n\\n### ENV variables tied to path\\n```dockerfile\\nENV PYENV_ROOT=\\\"/home/sandbox/.pyenv\\\"\\nENV CARGO_HOME=\\\"/home/sandbox/.cargo\\\"\\nENV SDKMAN_DIR=\\\"/home/sandbox/.sdkman\\\"\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 💡 Proposed Solution\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 🔧 1. Introduce `/workspace` as canonical HOME\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nENV HOME=/workspace\\nWORKDIR /workspace\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 🔧 2. Create shared group + user\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nRUN groupadd -g 1000 sandbox \\\\\\n && useradd -m -u 1000 -g sandbox -d /workspace -s /bin/bash sandbox\\n```\\n\\n👉 Important:\\n- user home MUST be `/workspace` (not `/home/sandbox`)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 🔧 3. Create and configure `/workspace`\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nRUN mkdir -p /workspace \\\\\\n && chown -R sandbox:sandbox /workspace \\\\\\n && chmod 2775 /workspace\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 🔧 4. Enforce ACL (critical)\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nRUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y acl\\n\\nRUN setfacl -R -m g:sandbox:rwx /workspace \\\\\\n && setfacl -d -m g:sandbox:rwx /workspace\\n```\\n\\n👉 Ensures:\\n- no umask issues\\n- all future files are group-accessible\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 🔧 5. Move all toolchains to `/workspace`\\n\\n### Replace:\\n\\n```dockerfile\\n/home/sandbox/.pyenv\\n```\\n\\n### With:\\n\\n```dockerfile\\n/workspace/.pyenv\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Required updates:\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nENV PYENV_ROOT=\\\"/workspace/.pyenv\\\"\\nENV CARGO_HOME=\\\"/workspace/.cargo\\\"\\nENV SDKMAN_DIR=\\\"/workspace/.sdkman\\\"\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### And COPY paths:\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nCOPY --from=python-stage /workspace/.pyenv /workspace/.pyenv\\nCOPY --from=rust-stage /workspace/.cargo /workspace/.cargo\\nCOPY --from=java-stage /workspace/.sdkman /workspace/.sdkman\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 🔧 6. Switch runtime user\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nUSER sandbox\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 🧪 Downstream Usage (hive-mind)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## ✅ Clean usage (no hacks)\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nRUN useradd -m -d /workspace -s /bin/bash hive \\\\\\n && usermod -aG sandbox hive\\n\\nUSER hive\\nENV HOME=/workspace\\nWORKDIR /workspace\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## ❌ No longer needed\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nmv /home/sandbox /home/hive\\nchown -R ...\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 🟡 Optional (temporary branding via symlink)\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nRUN ln -s /workspace /home/hive\\n```\\n\\n👉 Not required, only for compatibility\\n\\n---\\n\\n# ⚠️ Critical Things to Verify\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. `/etc/passwd` alignment\\n\\nUsers MUST have:\\n\\n```\\nhome directory = /workspace\\n```\\n\\nOtherwise:\\n- some tools ignore `$HOME`\\n- use passwd entry instead\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. No hardcoded `/home/sandbox` remains\\n\\nSearch entire repo for:\\n\\n```\\n/home/sandbox\\n```\\n\\nMust be replaced everywhere:\\n- Dockerfiles\\n- scripts\\n- ENV variables\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Tool compatibility\\n\\nVerify tools use `$HOME`:\\n\\n- `gh` → `$HOME/.config/gh`\\n- `npm` → `$HOME/.npm`\\n- `pip` → `$HOME/.local`\\n- `cargo` → `$HOME/.cargo`\\n\\n👉 Expected: ✅ all work correctly\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. Permissions\\n\\nEnsure:\\n\\n```bash\\n/workspace = drwxrwsr-x\\n```\\n\\nNOT:\\n- `755` ❌\\n- `777` ❌\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. ACL installed in all images\\n\\nAll images must include:\\n\\n```dockerfile\\napt-get install -y acl\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. No later layer overrides permissions\\n\\nWatch for:\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nCOPY ...\\n```\\n\\n👉 May reset ownership → must reapply if needed\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 7. Multi-stage consistency\\n\\nAll stages must use `/workspace`:\\n\\n- python stage\\n- rust stage\\n- java stage\\n- final stage\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 🧠 Why this is better\\n\\n---\\n\\n## ✅ Eliminates identity coupling\\n- no `/home/<user>` dependency\\n\\n---\\n\\n## ✅ No recursive operations\\n- no `chown -R`\\n- no `mv`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## ✅ Better container design\\n- aligns with:\\n  - `/workspace`\\n  - `/app`\\n  - `/data`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## ✅ Enables multi-user environments\\n- multiple users share same workspace\\n- no conflicts\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 🔥 Impact\\n\\n### Before\\n- slow builds\\n- fragile hacks\\n- broken permissions\\n\\n### After\\n- instant downstream builds\\n- predictable behavior\\n- clean architecture\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 🚀 Optional Improvements\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nENV XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/workspace/.config\\nENV XDG_CACHE_HOME=/workspace/.cache\\n```\\n\\n👉 Ensures all tools stay inside `/workspace`\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 📌 Conclusion\\n\\n> The current issue is caused by coupling **user identity with filesystem layout**\\n\\n### Fix by:\\n- moving to `/workspace`\\n- making it shared\\n- enforcing group + ACL\\n\\n---\\n\\n# 🏁 Recommendation\\n\\n- ✅ Migrate `/home/sandbox` → `/workspace`\\n- ✅ Apply group + ACL model\\n- ✅ Update all Docker stages\\n- ❌ Avoid renaming directories in downstream\",\"comments\":[]}",

# Handle negative values (can happen due to cleanup removing more than installed)\n171\t    if [ \"$diff_bytes\" -lt 0 ]; then\n172\t      diff_bytes=0\n173\t    fi\n174\t\n175\t    # Convert to MB with decimal precision (1 MB = 1,000,000 bytes).\n176\t    # Integer floor division via / 1024 / 1024 truncates any component under\n177\t    # 1 MiB to 0 MB, which is misleading (e.g. 741 KB shows as 0 MB).\n178\t    # Use awk for floating-point division to get precise values (e.g. 0.74 MB).\n179\t    # See docs/case-studies/issue-55 for full root cause analysis.\n180\t    local diff_mb\n181\t    diff_mb=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $diff_bytes / 1000000}\")\n182\t\n183\t    add_measurement \"$name\" \"$category\" \"$diff_bytes\" \"$diff_mb\"\n184\t    return 0\n185\t  else\n186\t    log_warning \"Installation of $name failed\"\n187\t    add_measurement \"$name\" \"$category\" 0 0\n188\t    return 1\n189\t  fi\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t# Measure apt package installation\n193\tmeasure_apt_install() {\n194\t  local name=\"$1\"\n195\t  local category=\"$2\"\n196\t  shift 2\n197\t  local packages=\"$*\"\n198\t\n199\t  measure_install \"$name\" \"$category\" maybe_sudo apt install -y $packages\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\t# ============================================================================\n203\t# MAIN MEASUREMENT SCRIPT\n204\t# ============================================================================\n205\t\n206\tlog_step \"Starting Disk Space Measurement\"\n207\tlog_note \"Results will be saved to: $JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\"\n208\t\n209\t# Initialize output\n210\tinit_json_output\n211\t\n212\t# Record baseline\n213\tcleanup_for_measurement\n214\tBASELINE_MB=$(get_disk_usage_mb)\n215\tlog_info \"Baseline disk usage: ${BASELINE_MB}MB\"\n216\t\n217\t# --- Pre-flight Checks ---\n218\tlog_step \"Running pre-flight checks\"\n219\t\n220\tif [ \"$EUID\" -ne 0 ] && ! sudo -n true 2>/dev/null; then\n221\t  log_error \"This script requires sudo access.\"\n222\t  exit 1\n223\tfi\n224\t\n225\tlog_success \"Pre-flight checks passed\"\n226\t\n227\t# --- Create sandbox user if missing ---\n228\tif ! id \"sandbox\" &>/dev/null; then\n229\t  log_info \"Creating sandbox user...\"\n230\t  useradd -m -s /bin/bash sandbox 2>/dev/null || adduser --disabled-password --gecos \"\" sandbox\n231\t  passwd -d sandbox 2>/dev/null || true\n232\t  usermod -aG sudo sandbox 2>/dev/null || true\n233\tfi\n234\t\n235\t# --- Prepare APT ---\n236\tlog_step \"Preparing APT sources\"\n237\tmaybe_sudo apt update -y || true\n238\tcleanup_for_measurement\n239\t\n240\t# ============================================================================\n241\t# SYSTEM PREREQUISITES (measured as a group)\n242\t# ============================================================================\n243\tlog_step \"Measuring System Prerequisites\"\n244\t\n245\tmeasure_apt_install \"Essential Tools\" \"System\" \\\n246\t  wget curl unzip zip git sudo ca-certificates gnupg build-essential expect screen\n247\t\n248\t# ============================================================================\n249\t# PROGRAMMING LANGUAGES - APT PACKAGES\n250\t# ============================================================================\n251\tlog_step \"Measuring APT-based Languages and Tools\"\n252\t\n253\tmeasure_apt_install \".NET SDK 8.0\" \"Runtime\" dotnet-sdk-8.0\n254\tmeasure_apt_install \"C/C++ Tools (CMake, Clang, LLVM, LLD)\" \"Build Tools\" cmake clang llvm lld\n255\tmeasure_apt_install \"Assembly Tools (NASM, FASM)\" \"Build Tools\" nasm fasm\n256\tmeasure_apt_install \"R Language\" \"Runtime\" r-base\n257\tmeasure_apt_install \"Bubblewrap\" \"Dependencies\" bubblewrap\n258\tmeasure_apt_install \"Ruby Build Dependencies\" \"Dependencies\" libyaml-dev\n259\tmeasure_apt_install \"Python Build Dependencies\" \"Dependencies\" \\\n260\t  libssl-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev libreadline-dev libsqlite3-dev \\\n261\t  libncursesw5-dev xz-utils tk-dev libxml2-dev libxmlsec1-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev\n262\t\n263\t# ============================================================================\n264\t# GitHub CLI\n265\t# ============================================================================\n266\tlog_step \"Measuring GitHub CLI\"\n267\t\n268\tinstall_gh_cli() {\n269\t  maybe_sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings\n270\t  local out\n271\t  out=$(mktemp)\n272\t  wget -nv -O\"$out\" https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg\n273\t  cat \"$out\" | maybe_sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg > /dev/null\n274\t  maybe_sudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg\n275\t  rm -f \"$out\"\n276\t  maybe_sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/sources.list.d\n277\t  echo \"deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main\" \\\n278\t    | maybe_sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null\n279\t  maybe_sudo apt update -y\n280\t  maybe_sudo apt install -y gh\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\tmeasure_install \"GitHub CLI\" \"Development Tools\" install_gh_cli\n284\t\n285\t# ============================================================================\n286\t# GitLab CLI\n287\t# ============================================================================\n288\tlog_step \"Measuring GitLab CLI\"\n289\t\n290\tmeasure_apt_install \"GitLab CLI\" \"Development Tools\" glab\n291\t\n292\t# ============================================================================\n293\t# Homebrew Directory Preparation\n294\t# ============================================================================\n295\tlog_step \"Preparing Homebrew Directory\"\n296\t\n297\tif [ ! -d /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew ]; then\n298\t  maybe_sudo mkdir -p /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew\n299\t  if id \"sandbox\" &>/dev/null; then\n300\t    maybe_sudo chown -R sandbox:sandbox /home/linuxbrew\n301\t  fi\n302\tfi\n303\t\n304\t# ============================================================================\n305\t# SANDBOX USER INSTALLATIONS\n306\t# The following tools are installed as the sandbox user\n307\t# ============================================================================\n308\tlog_step \"Measuring Sandbox User Installations\"\n309\t\n310\t# Create measurement script for sandbox user\n311\tcat > /tmp/sandbox-measure.sh << 'EOF_SANDBOX'\n312\t#!/usr/bin/env bash\n313\tset -euo pipefail\n314\t\n315\tJSON_OUTPUT_FILE=\"${1:-/tmp/disk-space-measurements.json}\"\n316\t\n317\t# Logging\n318\tlog_info() { echo -e \"\\033[0;34m[*]\\033[0m $1\"; }\n319\tlog_success() { echo -e \"\\033[0;32m[✓]\\033[0m $1\"; }\n320\tlog_warning() { echo -e \"\\033[1;33m[!]\\033[0m $1\"; }\n321\t\n322\tcommand_exists() { command -v \"$1\" &>/dev/null; }\n323\t\n324\tmaybe_sudo() {\n325\t  if [ \"$EUID\" -eq 0 ]; then \"$@\"\n326\t  elif command_exists sudo; then sudo \"$@\"\n327\t  else \"$@\"\n328\t  fi\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\tget_disk_usage_bytes() {\n332\t  df / --output=used --block-size=1 | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]'\n333\t}\n334\t\n335\tcleanup_for_measurement() {\n336\t  # Clean apt cache but preserve package lists (see docs/case-studies/issue-29)\n337\t  maybe_sudo apt-get clean 2>/dev/null || true\n338\t  rm -rf /tmp/measure-* 2>/dev/null || true\n339\t  sync\n340\t}\n341\t\n342\tadd_measurement() {\n343\t  local name=\"$1\"\n344\t  local category=\"$2\"\n345\t  local size_bytes=\"$3\"\n346\t  local size_mb=\"$4\"\n347\t\n348\t  # Use Python for reliable JSON manipulation, matching the outer script.\n349\t  # sed-based approaches fail silently on compact (single-line) JSON produced by\n350\t  # python3's json.dump(), because the pattern \\]$ does not match when the line\n351\t  # ends with } (the root object close) rather than ] (the array close).\n352\t  # See docs/case-studies/issue-49 for the full root cause analysis.\n353\t  python3 -c \"\n354\timport json, sys\n355\twith open('$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE', 'r') as f:\n356\t    data = json.load(f)\n357\tdata['components'].append({\n358\t    'name': sys.argv[1],\n359\t    'category': sys.argv[2],\n360\t    'size_bytes': int(sys.argv[3]),\n361\t    'size_mb': float(sys.argv[4])\n362\t})\n363\twith open('$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE', 'w') as f:\n364\t    json.dump(data, f)\n365\t\" \"$name\" \"$category\" \"$size_bytes\" \"$size_mb\"\n366\t\n367\t  log_success \"Recorded: $name - ${size_mb}MB\"\n368\t}\n369\t\n370\tmeasure_install() {\n371\t  local name=\"$1\"\n372\t  local category=\"$2\"\n373\t  shift 2\n374\t\n375\t  log_info \"Measuring: $name\"\n376\t  cleanup_for_measurement\n377\t  local start_bytes\n378\t  start_bytes=$(get_disk_usage_bytes)\n379\t\n380\t  if \"$@\"; then\n381\t    cleanup_for_measurement\n382\t    local end_bytes\n383\t    end_bytes=$(get_disk_usage_bytes)\n384\t    local diff_bytes=$((end_bytes - start_bytes))\n385\t    [ \"$diff_bytes\" -lt 0 ] && diff_bytes=0\n386\t    # Convert to MB with decimal precision. Use awk for floating-point division\n387\t    # to get precise values (e.g. 0.74 MB instead of 0 or 1).\n388\t    # See docs/case-studies/issue-55.\n389\t    local diff_mb\n390\t    diff_mb=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $diff_bytes / 1000000}\")\n391\t    add_measurement \"$name\" \"$category\" \"$diff_bytes\" \"$diff_mb\"\n392\t  else\n393\t    log_warning \"Installation of $name failed\"\n394\t    add_measurement \"$name\" \"$category\" 0 0\n395\t  fi\n396\t}\n397\t\n398\t# --- Bun ---\n399\tinstall_bun() {\n400\t  curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash\n401\t  export BUN_INSTALL=\"$HOME/.bun\"\n402\t  export PATH=\"$BUN_INSTALL/bin:$PATH\"\n403\t}\n404\tmeasure_install \"Bun\" \"Runtime\" install_bun\n405\t\n406\t# Ensure bun is in PATH for subsequent installs\n407\texport BUN_INSTALL=\"$HOME/.bun\"\n408\texport PATH=\"$BUN_INSTALL/bin:$PATH\"\n409\t\n410\t# --- gh-setup-git-identity ---\n411\tinstall_gh_identity() {\n412\t  bun install -g gh-setup-git-identity\n413\t}\n414\tmeasure_install \"gh-setup-git-identity\" \"Development Tools\" install_gh_identity\n415\t\n416\t# --- glab-setup-git-identity ---\n417\tinstall_glab_identity() {\n418\t  bun install -g glab-setup-git-identity\n419\t}\n420\tmeasure_install \"glab-setup-git-identity\" \"Development Tools\" install_glab_identity\n421\t\n422\t# --- Deno ---\n423\tinstall_deno() {\n424\t  curl -fsSL https://deno.land/install.sh | sh -s -- -y\n425\t  export DENO_INSTALL=\"$HOME/.deno\"\n426\t  export PATH=\"$DENO_INSTALL/bin:$PATH\"\n427\t}\n428\tmeasure_install \"Deno\" \"Runtime\" install_deno\n429\t\n430\t# --- NVM + Node.js ---\n431\tinstall_nvm_node() {\n432\t  curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.40.3/install.sh | bash\n433\t  export NVM_DIR=\"$HOME/.nvm\"\n434\t  [ -s \"$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh\" ] && \\. \"$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh\"\n435\t  nvm install 20\n436\t  npm install -g npm@latest --no-fund --silent\n437\t}\n438\tmeasure_install \"NVM + Node.js 20\" \"Runtime\" install_nvm_node\n439\t\n440\t# Load NVM for subsequent commands\n441\texport NVM_DIR=\"$HOME/.nvm\"\n442\t[ -s \"$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh\" ] && \\. \"$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh\"\n443\t\n444\t# --- Pyenv + Python ---\n445\tinstall_pyenv_python() {\n446\t  curl https://pyenv.run | bash\n447\t  export PYENV_ROOT=\"$HOME/.pyenv\"\n448\t  export PATH=\"$PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH\"\n449\t  eval \"$(pyenv init --path)\"\n450\t  eval \"$(pyenv init -)\"\n451\t\n452\t  LATEST_PYTHON=$(pyenv install --list | grep -E '^\\s*[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$' | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]')\n453\t  if [ -n \"$LATEST_PYTHON\" ]; then\n454\t    pyenv install \"$LATEST_PYTHON\"\n455\t    pyenv global \"$LATEST_PYTHON\"\n456\t  fi\n457\t}\n458\tmeasure_install \"Pyenv + Python (latest)\" \"Runtime\" install_pyenv_python\n459\t\n460\t# Load pyenv\n461\texport PYENV_ROOT=\"$HOME/.pyenv\"\n462\texport PATH=\"$PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH\"\n463\teval \"$(pyenv init --path)\" 2>/dev/null || true\n464\teval \"$(pyenv init -)\" 2>/dev/null || true\n465\t\n466\t# --- Golang ---\n467\tinstall_golang() {\n468\t  ARCH=$(uname -m)\n469\t  case \"$ARCH\" in\n470\t    x86_64) GO_ARCH=\"amd64\" ;;\n471\t    aarch64) GO_ARCH=\"arm64\" ;;\n472\t    *) GO_ARCH=\"\" ;;\n473\t  esac\n474\t\n475\t  if [ -n \"$GO_ARCH\" ]; then\n476\t    GO_VERSION=$(curl -sL 'https://go.dev/VERSION?m=text' | head -n1)\n477\t    GO_TARBALL=\"${GO_VERSION}.linux-${GO_ARCH}.tar.gz\"\n478\t    GO_URL=\"https://go.dev/dl/${GO_TARBALL}\"\n479\t\n480\t    TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)\n481\t    curl -sL \"$GO_URL\" -o \"$TEMP_DIR/$GO_TARBALL\"\n482\t    mkdir -p \"$HOME/.go\"\n483\t    tar -xzf \"$TEMP_DIR/$GO_TARBALL\" -C \"$HOME/.go\" --strip-components=1\n484\t    rm -rf \"$TEMP_DIR\"\n485\t\n486\t    export GOROOT=\"$HOME/.go\"\n487\t    export GOPATH=\"$HOME/.go/path\"\n488\t    export PATH=\"$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH\"\n489\t    mkdir -p \"$GOPATH\"\n490\t  fi\n491\t}\n492\tmeasure_install \"Go (latest)\" \"Runtime\" install_golang\n493\t\n494\t# Load Go\n495\texport GOROOT=\"$HOME/.go\"\n496\texport GOPATH=\"$HOME/.go/path\"\n497\texport PATH=\"$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH\"\n498\t\n499\t# --- Rust ---\n500\tinstall_rust() {\n501\t  curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y\n502\t  [ -f \"$HOME/.cargo/env\" ] && \\. \"$HOME/.cargo/env\"\n503\t}\n504\t# Use du-based measurement for Rust because the df-delta method is unreliable:\n505\t# rustup downloads files to /tmp then moves them to ~/.rustup and ~/.cargo,\n506\t# which can show near-zero df delta due to move-vs-copy on same filesystem.\n507\t# See docs/case-studies/issue-55 for full analysis.\n508\tcleanup_for_measurement\n509\tif install_rust; then\n510\t  cleanup_for_measurement\n511\t  # Only pass paths that exist to du; passing a non-existent path causes du to\n512\t  # exit with code 1, which kills the script under set -euo pipefail even though\n513\t  # stderr is redirected. See docs/case-studies/issue-57 for root cause analysis.\n514\t  rust_paths=()\n515\t  [[ -d \"$HOME/.rustup\" ]] && rust_paths+=(\"$HOME/.rustup\")\n516\t  [[ -d \"$HOME/.cargo\" ]] && rust_paths+=(\"$HOME/.cargo\")\n517\t  if [[ ${#rust_paths[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then\n518\t    rust_bytes=$(du -sb \"${rust_paths[@]}\" 2>/dev/null | awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum+0}')\n519\t  else\n520\t    rust_bytes=0\n521\t  fi\n522\t  rust_mb=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $rust_bytes / 1000000}\")\n523\t  add_measurement \"Rust (via rustup)\" \"Runtime\" \"$rust_bytes\" \"$rust_mb\"\n524\telse\n525\t  log_warning \"Installation of Rust (via rustup) failed\"\n526\t  add_measurement \"Rust (via rustup)\" \"Runtime\" 0 0\n527\tfi\n528\t\n529\t# Load Rust\n530\t[ -f \"$HOME/.cargo/env\" ] && \\. \"$HOME/.cargo/env\"\n531\t\n532\t# --- SDKMAN + Java ---\n533\tinstall_sdkman_java() {\n534\t  curl -s \"https://get.sdkman.io?rcupdate=false&ci=true\" | bash\n535\t  export SDKMAN_DIR=\"$HOME/.sdkman\"\n536\t  set +u\n537\t  [ -s \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\" ] && source \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\"\n538\t  sdk install java 21-tem < /dev/null || sdk install java 21-open < /dev/null || true\n539\t  set -u\n540\t}\n541\tmeasure_install \"SDKMAN + Java 21\" \"Runtime\" install_sdkman_java\n542\t\n543\t# Load SDKMAN\n544\texport SDKMAN_DIR=\"$HOME/.sdkman\"\n545\tset +u\n546\t[ -s \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\" ] && source \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\"\n547\tset -u\n548\t\n549\t# --- Kotlin ---\n550\tinstall_kotlin() {\n551\t  set +u\n552\t  sdk install kotlin < /dev/null || true\n553\t  set -u\n554\t}\n555\tmeasure_install \"Kotlin (via SDKMAN)\" \"Runtime\" install_kotlin\n556\t\n557\t# --- Lean ---\n558\tinstall_lean() {\n559\t  curl https://elan.lean-lang.org/elan-init.sh -sSf | sh -s -- -y --default-toolchain stable\n560\t  [ -f \"$HOME/.elan/env\" ] && \\. \"$HOME/.elan/env\"\n561\t}\n562\tmeasure_install \"Lean (via elan)\" \"Runtime\" install_lean\n563\t\n564\t# Load Lean\n565\t[ -f \"$HOME/.elan/env\" ] && \\. \"$HOME/.elan/env\"\n566\t\n567\t# --- Opam + Rocq ---\n568\tinstall_opam_rocq() {\n569\t  sudo apt install -y bubblewrap 2>/dev/null || true\n570\t  bash -c \"sh <(curl -fsSL https://opam.ocaml.org/install.sh) --no-backup\" <<< \"y\" || sudo apt install -y opam || true\n571\t\n572\t  if command -v opam &>/dev/null; then\n573\t    opam init --disable-sandboxing --auto-setup -y || true\n574\t    eval \"$(opam env --switch=default 2>/dev/null)\" || true\n575\t    opam repo add rocq-released https://rocq-prover.org/opam/released 2>/dev/null || true\n576\t    opam update 2>/dev/null || true\n577\t    opam pin add rocq-prover --yes 2>/dev/null || opam install rocq-prover -y 2>/dev/null || opam install coq -y 2>/dev/null || true\n578\t  fi\n579\t}\n580\tmeasure_install \"Opam + Rocq/Coq\" \"Runtime\" install_opam_rocq\n581\t\n582\t# --- Homebrew ---\n583\tinstall_homebrew() {\n584\t  NONINTERACTIVE=1 /bin/bash -c \"$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)\" || true\n585\t\n586\t  if [[ -x /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew ]]; then\n587\t    eval \"$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)\"\n588\t  elif [[ -x \"$HOME/.linuxbrew/bin/brew\" ]]; then\n589\t    eval \"$(\"$HOME/.linuxbrew/bin/brew\" shellenv)\"\n590\t  fi\n591\t}\n592\t# Use du-based measurement for Homebrew because the outer script pre-creates\n593\t# /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew before this runs, making the df-delta near-zero.\n594\t# See docs/case-studies/issue-55 for full analysis.\n595\tcleanup_for_measurement\n596\tif install_homebrew; then\n597\t  cleanup_for_measurement\n598\t  # Only pass paths that exist to du; passing a non-existent path causes du to\n599\t  # exit with code 1, which kills the script under set -euo pipefail even though\n600\t  # stderr is redirected. See docs/case-studies/issue-57 for root cause analysis.\n601\t  brew_paths=()\n602\t  [[ -d /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew ]] && brew_paths+=(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew)\n603\t  [[ -d \"$HOME/.linuxbrew\" ]] && brew_paths+=(\"$HOME/.linuxbrew\")\n604\t  if [[ ${#brew_paths[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then\n605\t    brew_bytes=$(du -sb \"${brew_paths[@]}\" 2>/dev/null | awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum+0}')\n606\t  else\n607\t    brew_bytes=0\n608\t  fi\n609\t  brew_mb=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $brew_bytes / 1000000}\")\n610\t  add_measurement \"Homebrew\" \"Package Manager\" \"$brew_bytes\" \"$brew_mb\"\n611\telse\n612\t  log_warning \"Installation of Homebrew failed\"\n613\t  add_measurement \"Homebrew\" \"Package Manager\" 0 0\n614\tfi\n615\t\n616\t# Load Homebrew\n617\tif [[ -x /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew ]]; then\n618\t  eval \"$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)\"\n619\telif [[ -x \"$HOME/.linuxbrew/bin/brew\" ]]; then\n620\t  eval \"$(\"$HOME/.linuxbrew/bin/brew\" shellenv)\"\n621\tfi\n622\t\n623\t# --- PHP ---\n624\tinstall_php() {\n625\t  if command -v brew &>/dev/null; then\n626\t    brew tap shivammathur/php || true\n627\t    export HOMEBREW_NO_ANALYTICS=1\n628\t    export HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1\n629\t    brew install shivammathur/php/php@8.3 || true\n630\t    brew link --overwrite --force shivammathur/php/php@8.3 2>&1 | grep -v \"Warning\" || true\n631\t  fi\n632\t}\n633\tmeasure_install \"PHP 8.3 (via Homebrew)\" \"Runtime\" install_php\n634\t\n635\t# --- Perlbrew + Perl ---\n636\tinstall_perlbrew_perl() {\n637\t  export PERLBREW_ROOT=\"$HOME/.perl5\"\n638\t  curl -L https://install.perlbrew.pl | bash\n639\t\n640\t  if [ -f \"$PERLBREW_ROOT/etc/bashrc\" ]; then\n641\t    sed -i 's/\\$1/${1:-}/g' \"$PERLBREW_ROOT/etc/bashrc\" 2>/dev/null || true\n642\t    sed -i 's/\\$PERLBREW_LIB/${PERLBREW_LIB:-}/g' \"$PERLBREW_ROOT/etc/bashrc\" 2>/dev/null || true\n643\t    sed -i 's/\\$outsep/${outsep:-}/g' \"$PERLBREW_ROOT/etc/bashrc\" 2>/dev/null || true\n644\t\n645\t    set +u\n646\t    source \"$PERLBREW_ROOT/etc/bashrc\"\n647\t    set -u\n648\t\n649\t    LATEST_PERL=$(perlbrew available 2>&1 | grep -oE 'perl-5\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+' | head -1 || true)\n650\t    if [ -n \"$LATEST_PERL\" ]; then\n651\t      perlbrew install \"$LATEST_PERL\" --notest || true\n652\t      perlbrew switch \"$LATEST_PERL\" || true\n653\t    fi\n654\t  fi\n655\t}\n656\tmeasure_install \"Perlbrew + Perl (latest)\" \"Runtime\" install_perlbrew_perl\n657\t\n658\t# --- rbenv + Ruby ---\n659\tinstall_rbenv_ruby() {\n660\t  git clone https://github.com/rbenv/rbenv.git \"$HOME/.rbenv\"\n661\t  mkdir -p \"$HOME/.rbenv/plugins\"\n662\t  git clone https://github.com/rbenv/ruby-build.git \"$HOME/.rbenv/plugins/ruby-build\"\n663\t\n664\t  export PATH=\"$HOME/.rbenv/bin:$PATH\"\n665\t  eval \"$(rbenv init - bash)\"\n666\t\n667\t  LATEST_RUBY=$(rbenv install -l 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^\\s*3\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$' | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]')\n668\t  if [ -n \"$LATEST_RUBY\" ]; Relative\n710\t# paths like 'data/disk-space-measurements.json' would then resolve to the wrong\n711\t# location, causing \"No such file or directory\" errors.\n712\t# See docs/case-studies/issue-46 for the full root cause analysis.\n713\tJSON_OUTPUT_FILE_ABS=\"$(realpath \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\")\"\n714\t# The sandbox user needs to read and write the JSON file, but the GitHub Actions\n715\t# workspace directories (e.g. /home/runner/work/sandbox/sandbox/) are owned by\n716\t# 'runner' and typically have mode 750, so 'sandbox' (not in the 'runner' group)\n717\t# cannot traverse them even if the file itself is world-readable.\n718\t# Solution: copy the JSON file to /tmp (world-accessible, mode 1777), run the\n719\t# sandbox measurements against that copy, then copy the result back.\n720\tJSON_TMP_COPY=\"$(mktemp /tmp/disk-space-measurements-XXXXXX.json)\"\n721\tcp \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE_ABS\" \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\n722\tchmod o+rw \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\n723\t\n724\t# Execute sandbox user measurements against the /tmp copy\n725\tif [ \"$EUID\" -eq 0 ]; then\n726\t  su - sandbox -c \"bash /tmp/sandbox-measure.sh '$JSON_TMP_COPY'\"\n727\telse\n728\t  sudo -i -u sandbox bash /tmp/sandbox-measure.sh \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\n729\tfi\n730\t\n731\t# Copy the updated measurements back to the original location\n732\tcp \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\" \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE_ABS\"\n733\trm -f \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\n734\t\n735\trm -f /tmp/sandbox-measure.sh\n736\t\n737\t# ============================================================================\n738\t# FINALIZE\n739\t# ============================================================================\n740\tlog_step \"Finalizing Measurements\"\n741\t\n742\tcleanup_for_measurement\n743\tFINAL_MB=$(get_disk_usage_mb)\n744\tTOTAL_MB=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $FINAL_MB - $BASELINE_MB}\")\n745\t\n746\tfinalize_json_output \"$TOTAL_MB\"\n747\t\n748\tlog_step \"Measurement Complete!\"\n749\tlog_success \"Total installation size: ${TOTAL_MB}MB\"\n750\tlog_success \"Results saved to: $JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\"\n751\t\n752\t# Print summary\n753\techo \"\"\n754\techo \"=== DISK SPACE SUMMARY ===\"\n755\tcat \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\" | python3 -c \"\n756\timport json, sys\n757\tdata = json.load(sys.stdin)\n758\tprint(f\\\"Generated: {data['generated_at']}\\\")\n759\tprint(f\\\"Total Size: {data['total_size_mb']}MB\\\")\n760\tprint()\n761\tprint('Components by Category:')\n762\tcategories = {}\n763\tfor comp in data['components']:\n764\t    cat = comp['category']\n765\t    if cat not in categories:\n766\t        categories[cat] = []\n767\t    categories[cat].append(comp)\n768\t\n769\tfor cat, comps in sorted(categories.items()):\n770\t    print(f'  {cat}:')\n771\t    for c in sorted(comps, key=lambda x: x['size_mb'], reverse=True):\n772\t        print(f\\\"    {c['name']}: {c['size_mb']}MB\\\")\n773\t\" 2>/dev/null || cat \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\"\n774\t"

then\n  JSON_OUTPUT_FILE=\"$2\"\nfi\n\n# Color codes for enhanced output (disabled in non-TTY)\nif [ -t 1 ]; then\n  RED='\\033[0;31m'\n  GREEN='\\033[0;32m'\n  YELLOW='\\033[1;33m'\n  BLUE='\\033[0;34m'\n  CYAN='\\033[0;36m'\n  NC='\\033[0m' # No Color\nelse\n  RED=''\n  GREEN=''\n  YELLOW=''\n  BLUE=''\n  CYAN=''\n  NC=''\nfi\n\n# Logging functions\nlog_info() { echo -e \"${BLUE}[*]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_success() { echo -e \"${GREEN}[✓]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_warning() { echo -e \"${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_error() { echo -e \"${RED}[✗]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_note() { echo -e \"${CYAN}[i]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_step() { echo -e \"\\n${GREEN}==>${NC} ${BLUE}$1${NC}\\n\"; }\n\n# Check if a command exists (silent)\ncommand_exists() {\n  command -v \"$1\" &>/dev/null\n}\n\n# Run command with sudo only if not root and sudo is available\nmaybe_sudo() {\n  if [ \"$EUID\" -eq 0 ]; then\n    \"$@\"\n  elif command_exists sudo; then\n    sudo \"$@\"\n  else\n    \"$@\"\n  fi\n}\n\n# Get current disk usage in bytes\nget_disk_usage_bytes() {\n  df / --output=used --block-size=1 | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]'\n}\n\n# Get current disk usage in MB (for display)\nget_disk_usage_mb() {\n  df / --output=used --block-size=1M | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]'\n}\n\n# Cleanup function to ensure accurate measurements\ncleanup_for_measurement() {\n  # Clean apt cache (downloaded .deb files) but preserve package lists\n  # NOTE: We intentionally do NOT delete /var/lib/apt/lists/* as it breaks\n  # package installation. sys.argv[2],\n    'size_bytes': int(sys.argv[3]),\n    'size_mb': float(sys.argv[4])\n})\nwith open('$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(data, f)\n\" \"$name\" \"$category\" \"$size_bytes\" \"Finalized JSON output with total: ${total_mb}MB\"\n}\n\n# Measure installation of a component\n# Args: component_name category install_command...\nmeasure_install() {\n  local name=\"$1\"\n  local category=\"$2\"\n  shift 2\n\n  log_info \"Measuring installation: $name\"\n\n  # Cleanup before measurement\n  cleanup_for_measurement\n\n  # Record starting disk usage\n  local start_bytes\n  start_bytes=$(get_disk_usage_bytes)\n\n  # Run installation command\n  if \"$@\"; then\n    # Cleanup after installation\n    cleanup_for_measurement\n\n    # Record ending disk usage\n    local end_bytes\n    end_bytes=$(get_disk_usage_bytes)\n\n    # Calculate difference\n    local diff_bytes=$((end_bytes - start_bytes))\n\n    # Handle negative values (can happen due to cleanup removing 1024 / 1024 truncates any component under\n    # 1 MiB to 0 MB, which is misleading (e.g. 741 KB shows as 0 MB).\n    # Use awk for floating-point division to get precise values (e.g. 0.74 MB).\n    # ---\ninstall_golang() {\n  ARCH=$(uname -m)\n  case \"$ARCH\" in\n    x86_64) GO_ARCH=\"amd64\" ;;\n    aarch64) GO_ARCH=\"arm64\" ;;\n    *) GO_ARCH=\"\" ;;\n  esac\n\n  if [ -n \"$GO_ARCH\" ]; \"$TEMP_DIR\"\n\n    export GOROOT=\"$HOME/.go\"\n    export GOPATH=\"$HOME/.go/path\"\n    export PATH=\"$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH\"\n    mkdir -p \"$GOPATH\"\n  fi\n}\nmeasure_install \"Go (latest)\" \"Runtime\" install_golang\n\n# Load Go\nexport GOROOT=\"$HOME/.go\"\nexport GOPATH=\"$HOME/.go/path\"\nexport PATH=\"$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH\"\n\n# --- Rust ---\ninstall_rust() {\n  curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y\n  [ -f \"$HOME/.cargo/env\" ] && \\. \"$HOME/.cargo/env\"\n}\n# Use du-based measurement for Rust because the df-delta method is unreliable:\n# rustup downloads files to /tmp then moves them to ~/.rustup and ~/.cargo,\n# which can show near-zero df delta due to move-vs-copy on same filesystem.\n# See docs/case-studies/issue-55 for full analysis.\ncleanup_for_measurement\nif install_rust; then\n  cleanup_for_measurement\n  # Only pass paths that exist to du; passing a non-existent path causes du to\n  # exit with code 1, which kills the script under set -euo pipefail even though\n  # stderr is redirected. See docs/case-studies/issue-57 for root cause analysis.\n  rust_paths=()\n  [[ -d \"$HOME/.rustup\" ]] && rust_paths+=(\"$HOME/.rustup\")\n  [[ -d \"$HOME/.cargo\" ]] && rust_paths+=(\"$HOME/.cargo\")\n  if [[ ${#rust_paths[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then\n    rust_bytes=$(du -sb \"${rust_paths[@]}\" 2>/dev/null | awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum+0}')\n  else\n    rust_bytes=0\n  fi\n  rust_mb=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $rust_bytes / 1000000}\")\n  add_measurement \"Rust (via rustup)\" \"Runtime\" \"$rust_bytes\" \"$rust_mb\"\nelse\n  log_warning \"Installation of Rust (via rustup) failed\"\n  add_measurement \"Rust (via rustup)\" \"Runtime\" 0 0\nfi\n\n# Load Rust\n[ -f \"$HOME/.cargo/env\" ] && \\. \"$HOME/.cargo/env\"\n\n# --- SDKMAN + Java ---\ninstall_sdkman_java() {\n  curl -s \"https://get.sdkman.io?rcupdate=false&ci=true\" | bash\n  export SDKMAN_DIR=\"$HOME/.sdkman\"\n  set +u\n  [ -s \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\" ] && source \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\"\n  sdk install java 21-tem < /dev/null || sdk install java 21-open < /dev/null || true\n  set -u\n}\nmeasure_install \"SDKMAN + Java 21\" \"Runtime\" install_sdkman_java\n\n# Load SDKMAN\nexport SDKMAN_DIR=\"$HOME/.sdkman\"\nset +u\n[ -s \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\" ] && source \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\"\nset -u\n\n# --- Kotlin ---\ninstall_kotlin() {\n  set +u\n  sdk install kotlin < /dev/null || true\n  set -u\n}\nmeasure_install \"Kotlin (via SDKMAN)\" \"Runtime\" install_kotlin\n\n# --- Lean ---\ninstall_lean() {\n  curl https://elan.lean-lang.org/elan-init.sh -sSf | sh -s -- -y --default-toolchain stable\n  [ -f \"$HOME/.elan/env\" ] && \\. \"$HOME/.elan/env\"\n}\nmeasure_install \"Lean (via elan)\" \"Runtime\" install_lean\n\n# Load Lean\n[ -f \"$HOME/.elan/env\" ] && \\. \"$HOME/.elan/env\"\n\n# --- Opam + Rocq ---\ninstall_opam_rocq() {\n  sudo apt install -y bubblewrap 2>/dev/null || true\n  bash -c \"sh <(curl -fsSL https://opam.ocaml.org/install.sh) --no-backup\" <<< \"y\" || sudo apt install -y opam || true\n\n  if command -v opam &>/dev/null; then\n    opam init --disable-sandboxing --auto-setup -y || true\n    eval \"$(opam env --switch=default 2>/dev/null)\" || true\n    opam repo add rocq-released https://rocq-prover.org/opam/released 2>/dev/null || true\n    opam update 2>/dev/null || true\n    opam pin add rocq-prover --yes 2>/dev/null || opam install rocq-prover -y 2>/dev/null || opam install coq -y 2>/dev/null || true\n  fi\n}\nmeasure_install \"Opam + Rocq/Coq\" \"Runtime\" install_opam_rocq\n\n# --- Homebrew ---\ninstall_homebrew() {\n  NONINTERACTIVE=1 /bin/bash -c \"$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)\" || true\n\n  if [[ -x /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew ]]; then\n    eval \"$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)\"\n  elif [[ -x \"$HOME/.linuxbrew/bin/brew\" ]]; then\n    eval \"$(\"$HOME/.linuxbrew/bin/brew\" shellenv)\"\n  fi\n}\n# Use du-based measurement for Homebrew because the outer script pre-creates\n# /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew before this runs, making the df-delta near-zero.\n# See docs/case-studies/issue-55 for full analysis.\ncleanup_for_measurement\nif install_homebrew; then\n  cleanup_for_measurement\n  # Only pass paths that exist to du; passing a non-existent path causes du to\n  # exit with code 1, which kills the script under set -euo pipefail even though\n  # stderr is redirected. See docs/case-studies/issue-57 for root cause analysis.\n  brew_paths=()\n  [[ -d /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew ]] && brew_paths+=(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew)\n  [[ -d \"$HOME/.linuxbrew\" ]] && brew_paths+=(\"$HOME/.linuxbrew\")\n  if [[ ${#brew_paths[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then\n    brew_bytes=$(du -sb \"${brew_paths[@]}\" 2>/dev/null | awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum+0}')\n  else\n    brew_bytes=0\n  fi\n  brew_mb=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $brew_bytes / 1000000}\")\n  add_measurement \"Homebrew\" \"Package Manager\" \"$brew_bytes\" \"$brew_mb\"\nelse\n  log_warning \"Installation of Homebrew failed\"\n  add_measurement \"Homebrew\" \"Package Manager\" 0 0\nfi\n\n# Load Homebrew\nif [[ -x /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew ]]; Relative\n# paths like 'data/disk-space-measurements.json' would then resolve to the wrong\n# location, causing \"No such file or directory\" errors.\n# See docs/case-studies/issue-46 for the full root cause analysis.\nJSON_OUTPUT_FILE_ABS=\"$(realpath \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\")\"\n# The sandbox user needs to read and write the JSON file, but the GitHub Actions\n# workspace directories (e.g. /home/runner/work/sandbox/sandbox/) are owned by\n# 'runner' and typically have mode 750, so 'sandbox' (not in the 'runner' group)\n# cannot traverse them even if the file itself is world-readable.\n# Solution: copy the JSON file to /tmp (world-accessible, mode 1777), run the\n# sandbox measurements against that copy, then copy the result back.\nJSON_TMP_COPY=\"$(mktemp /tmp/disk-space-measurements-XXXXXX.json)\"\ncp \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE_ABS\" \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\nchmod o+rw \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\n\n# Execute sandbox user measurements against the /tmp copy\nif [ \"$EUID\" -eq 0 ]; then\n  su - sandbox -c \"bash /tmp/sandbox-measure.sh '$JSON_TMP_COPY'\"\nelse\n  sudo -i -u sandbox bash /tmp/sandbox-measure.sh \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\nfi\n\n# Copy the updated measurements back to the original location\ncp \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\" \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE_ABS\"\nrm -f \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\n\nrm -f /tmp/sandbox-measure.sh\n\n# ============================================================================\n# FINALIZE\n# ============================================================================\nlog_step \"Finalizing Measurements\"\n\ncleanup_for_measurement\nFINAL_MB=$(get_disk_usage_mb)\nTOTAL_MB=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $FINAL_MB - $BASELINE_MB}\")\n\nfinalize_json_output \"$TOTAL_MB\"\n\nlog_step \"Measurement Complete!\"\nlog_success \"Total installation size: ${TOTAL_MB}MB\"\nlog_success \"Results saved to: $JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\"\n\n# Print summary\necho \"\"\necho \"=== DISK SPACE SUMMARY ===\"\ncat \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\" | python3 -c \"\nimport json, sys\ndata = json.load(sys.stdin)\nprint(f\\\"Generated: {data['generated_at']}\\\")\nprint(f\\\"Total Size: {data['total_size_mb']}MB\\\")\nprint()\nprint('Components by Category:')\ncategories = {}\nfor comp in data['components']:\n    cat = comp['category']\n    if cat not in categories:\n        categories[cat] = []\n    categories[cat].append(comp)\n\nfor cat, comps in sorted(categories.items()):\n    print(f'  {cat}:')\n    for c in sorted(comps, key=lambda x: x['size_mb'], reverse=True):\n        print(f\\\"    {c['name']}: {c['size_mb']}MB\\\")\n\" 2>/dev/null || cat \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\"\n",

}\nlog_success() { echo -e \"${GREEN}[✓]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_warning() { echo -e \"${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_error() { echo -e \"${RED}[✗]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_note() { echo -e \"${CYAN}[i]${NC} $1\"; }\nlog_step() { echo -e \"\\n${GREEN}==>${NC} ${BLUE}$1${NC}\\n\"; }\n\n# Check if a command exists (silent)\ncommand_exists() {\n  command -v \"$1\" &>/dev/null\n}\n\n# Run command with sudo only if not root and sudo is available\nmaybe_sudo() {\n  if [ \"$EUID\" -eq 0 ]; then\n    \"$@\"\n  elif command_exists sudo; then\n    sudo \"$@\"\n  else\n    \"$@\"\n  fi\n}\n\n# Get current disk usage in bytes\nget_disk_usage_bytes() {\n  df / --output=used --block-size=1 | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]'\n}\n\n# Get current disk usage in MB (for display)\nget_disk_usage_mb() {\n  df / --output=used --block-size=1M | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]'\n}\n\n# Cleanup function to ensure accurate measurements\ncleanup_for_measurement() {\n  # Clean apt cache (downloaded .deb files) but preserve package lists\n  # NOTE: We intentionally do NOT delete /var/lib/apt/lists/* as it breaks\n  # package installation. See docs/case-studies/issue-29 for details.\n  maybe_sudo apt-get clean 2>/dev/null || true\n\n  # Clean temp files\n  maybe_sudo rm -rf /tmp/* 2>/dev/null || true\n  maybe_sudo rm -rf /var/tmp/* 2>/dev/null || true\n\n  # Sync filesystem\n  sync\n}\n\n# Initialize JSON output\ninit_json_output() {\n  cat > \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\" << 'EOF'\n{\n  \"generated_at\": \"\",\n  \"total_size_mb\": 0,\n  \"components\": []\n}\nEOF\n  log_info \"Initialized JSON output at $JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\"\n}\n\n# Add component measurement to JSON\n# Uses Python for reliable JSON manipulation, avoiding sed issues with special\n# characters in component names (e.g., \"C/C++ Tools\"). sys.argv[2],\n    'size_bytes': int(sys.argv[3]),\n    'size_mb': float(sys.argv[4])\n})\nwith open('$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(data, f)\n\" \"$name\" \"$category\" \"$size_bytes\" \"$size_mb\"\n\n  log_success \"Recorded: $name - ${size_mb}MB\"\n}\n\n# Finalize JSON output with timestamp and total\n# Uses Python for reliable JSON manipulation.\nfinalize_json_output() {\n  local total_mb=\"$1\"\n\n  python3 -c \"\nimport json\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nwith open('$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE', 'r') as f:\n    data = json.load(f)\ndata['generated_at'] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')\ndata['total_size_mb'] = float('$total_mb')\nwith open('$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(data, f)\n\"\n\n  log_success \"Finalized JSON output with total: ${total_mb}MB\"\n}\n\n# Measure installation of a component\n# Args: component_name category install_command...\nmeasure_install() {\n  local name=\"$1\"\n  local category=\"$2\"\n  shift 2\n\n  log_info \"Measuring installation: $name\"\n\n  # Cleanup before measurement\n  cleanup_for_measurement\n\n  # Record starting disk usage\n  local start_bytes\n  start_bytes=$(get_disk_usage_bytes)\n\n  # Run installation command\n  if \"$@\"; then\n    # Cleanup after installation\n    cleanup_for_measurement\n\n    # Record ending disk usage\n    local end_bytes\n    end_bytes=$(get_disk_usage_bytes)\n\n    # Calculate difference\n    local diff_bytes=$((end_bytes - start_bytes))\n\n    # Handle negative values (can happen due to cleanup removing more than installed)\n    if [ \"$diff_bytes\" -lt 0 ]; then\n      diff_bytes=0\n    fi\n\n    # Convert to MB with decimal precision (1 MB = 1,000,000 bytes).\n    # Integer floor division via / 1024 / 1024 truncates any component under\n    # 1 MiB to 0 MB, which is misleading (e.g. 741 KB shows as 0 MB).\n    # Use awk for floating-point division to get precise values (e.g. 0.74 MB).\n    # See docs/case-studies/issue-55 for full root cause analysis.\n    local diff_mb\n    diff_mb=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $diff_bytes / 1000000}\")\n\n    add_measurement \"$name\" \"$category\" \"$diff_bytes\" \"$diff_mb\"\n    return 0\n  else\n    log_warning \"Installation of $name failed\"\n    add_measurement \"$name\" \"$category\" 0 0\n    return 1\n  fi\n}\n\n# Measure apt package installation\nmeasure_apt_install() {\n  local name=\"$1\"\n  local category=\"$2\"\n  shift 2\n  local packages=\"$*\"\n\n  measure_install \"$name\" \"$category\" maybe_sudo apt install -y $packages\n}\n\n# ============================================================================\n# MAIN MEASUREMENT SCRIPT\n# ============================================================================\n\nlog_step \"Starting Disk Space Measurement\"\nlog_note \"Results will be saved to: $JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\"\n\n# Initialize output\ninit_json_output\n\n# Record baseline\ncleanup_for_measurement\nBASELINE_MB=$(get_disk_usage_mb)\nlog_info \"Baseline disk usage: ${BASELINE_MB}MB\"\n\n# --- Pre-flight Checks ---\nlog_step passed\"\n\n# --- Create sandbox user if missing ---\nif ! id \"sandbox\" &>/dev/null; then\n  log_info \"Creating sandbox user...\"\n  useradd -m -s /bin/bash sandbox 2>/dev/null || adduser out=$(mktemp)\n  wget -nv -O\"$out\" https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg\n  cat \"$out\" | maybe_sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg > /dev/null\n sandbox:sandbox /home/linuxbrew\n  fi\nfi\n\n# ============================================================================\n# SANDBOX USER INSTALLATIONS\n# The following tools are installed as the /tmp/sandbox-measure.sh << 'EOF_SANDBOX'\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\nset -euo pipefail\n\nJSON_OUTPUT_FILE=\"${1:-/tmp/disk-space-measurements.json}\"\n\n# Logging\nlog_info() { echo -e \"\\033[0;34m[*]\\033[0m $1\"; }\nlog_success() { echo -e \"\\033[0;32m[✓]\\033[0m $1\"; }\nlog_warning() { echo -e \"\\033[1;33m[!]\\033[0m $1\"; }\n\ncommand_exists() { command -v \"$1\" &>/dev/null; }\n\nmaybe_sudo() {\n  if [ \"$EUID\" -eq 0 ]; then \"$@\"\n  elif command_exists sudo; then sudo \"$@\"\n  else \"$@\"\n  fi\n}\n\nget_disk_usage_bytes() {\n  df / --output=used --block-size=1 | tail -1 | tr -d '[:space:]'\n}\n\ncleanup_for_measurement() {\n  # Clean apt cache but preserve package lists (see docs/case-studies/issue-29)\n  maybe_sudo apt-get clean 2>/dev/null || true\n  rm -rf /tmp/measure-* 2>/dev/null || true\n  sync\n}\n\nadd_measurement() {\n  local name=\"$1\"\n  local category=\"$2\"\n  local size_bytes=\"$3\"\n  local size_mb=\"$4\"\n\n  # Use Python for reliable JSON manipulation, matching the outer script.\n  # sed-based approaches fail silently on compact (single-line) JSON produced by\n  # python3's json.dump(), because the pattern \\]$ does not match when the line\n  # ends with } (the root object close) rather than ] (the array close).\n  # See docs/case-studies/issue-49 for the full root cause analysis.\n  python3 -c \"\nimport json, sys\nwith open('$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE', 'r') as f:\n    data = json.load(f)\ndata['components'].append({\n    'name': sys.argv[1],\n    'category': sys.argv[2],\n    'size_bytes': int(sys.argv[3]),\n    'size_mb': float(sys.argv[4])\n})\nwith open('$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(data, f)\n\" \"$name\" \"$category\" \"$size_bytes\" \"$size_mb\"\n\n  log_success \"Recorded: $name - ${size_mb}MB\"\n}\n\nmeasure_install() {\n  local name=\"$1\"\n  local category=\"$2\"\n  shift 2\n\n  log_info \"Measuring: $name\"\n  cleanup_for_measurement\n  local start_bytes\n  start_bytes=$(get_disk_usage_bytes)\n\n  if \"$@\"; then\n    cleanup_for_measurement\n    local end_bytes\n    end_bytes=$(get_disk_usage_bytes)\n    local diff_bytes=$((end_bytes - start_bytes))\n    [ \"$diff_bytes\" -lt 0 ] && diff_bytes=0\n    # Convert to MB with decimal precision. Use awk for floating-point division\n    # to get precise values (e.g. 0.74 MB instead of 0 or 1).\n    # See docs/case-studies/issue-55.\n    local diff_mb\n    diff_mb=$(awk \"BEGIN {printf \\\"%.2f\\\", $diff_bytes / 1000000}\")\n    add_measurement \"$name\" \"$category\" \"$diff_bytes\" \"$diff_mb\"\n  else\n    log_warning \"Installation of $name failed\"\n    add_measurement \"$name\" \"$category\" 0 0\n  fi\n}\n\n# --- Bun ---\ninstall_bun() {\n  curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash\n  export BUN_INSTALL=\"$HOME/.bun\"\n  export PATH=\"$BUN_INSTALL/bin:$PATH\"\n}\nmeasure_install \"Bun\" \"Runtime\" install_bun\n\n# Ensure bun is in PATH for subsequent installs\nexport BUN_INSTALL=\"$HOME/.bun\"\nexport PATH=\"$BUN_INSTALL/bin:$PATH\"\n\n# --- gh-setup-git-identity ---\ninstall_gh_identity() {\n  bun install -g gh-setup-git-identity\n}\nmeasure_install \"gh-setup-git-identity\" \"Development Tools\" install_glab_identity\n\n# --- Deno ---\ninstall_deno() {\n  curl -fsSL https://deno.land/install.sh | sh -s -- -y\n  export DENO_INSTALL=\"$HOME/.deno\"\n  export \\. \"$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh\"\n\n# --- Pyenv + Python ---\ninstall_pyenv_python() {\n  curl https://pyenv.run | bash\n  export PYENV_ROOT=\"$HOME/.pyenv\"\n  export PATH=\"$PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH\"\n  eval is unreliable:\n# rustup downloads files to /tmp then moves them to ~/.rustup and ~/.cargo,\n# which can show near-zero df delta due to move-vs-copy on same filesystem.\n# See Relative\n# paths like 'data/disk-space-measurements.json' would then resolve to the wrong\n# location, causing \"No such file or directory\" errors.\n# See docs/case-studies/issue-46 for the full root cause analysis.\nJSON_OUTPUT_FILE_ABS=\"$(realpath \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE\")\"\n# The sandbox user needs to read and write the JSON file, but the GitHub Actions\n# workspace directories (e.g. /home/runner/work/sandbox/sandbox/) are owned by\n# 'runner' and typically have mode 750, so 'sandbox' (not in the 'runner' group)\n# cannot traverse them even if the file itself is world-readable.\n# Solution: copy the JSON file to /tmp (world-accessible, mode 1777), run the\n# sandbox measurements against that copy, then copy the result back.\nJSON_TMP_COPY=\"$(mktemp /tmp/disk-space-measurements-XXXXXX.json)\"\ncp \"$JSON_OUTPUT_FILE_ABS\" \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\nchmod o+rw \"$JSON_TMP_COPY\"\n\n# Execute sandbox user measurements against the /tmp copy\nif [ \"$EUID\" -eq 0 ]; Please run with sudo or ensure user has sudo privileges.\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n\n# Check Ubuntu version\nif [ -f /etc/os-release ]; then\n  source /etc/os-release\n  if [[ \"$ID\" != \"ubuntu\" ]]; then\n    log_warning \"This script is designed for Ubuntu. then\n  log_warning \"Low disk space detected: ${AVAILABLE_GB}GB available\"\n  log_warning \"Recommended: at least 15GB free space\"\nelse\n  log_success \"Sufficient disk space available: ${AVAILABLE_GB}GB\"\nfi\n\n# Check internet connectivity\nif ping -c 1 -W 5 google.com &>/dev/null;
